raw_note: S: Patient reports improved sleep and reduced anxiety over the past two weeks. O: Alert and oriented x3. Mood euthymic. A: Generalized anxiety disorder improving with CBT. P: Continue weekly sessions.
D: Patient discussed ongoing work-related stressors. A: Cognitive restructuring techniques applied. P: Daily mindfulness practice.
Initial intake. Patient presents with depression symptoms lasting 6 months. PHQ-9 score 14.
B: Patient late to session; reports increased cannabis use. I: Motivational interviewing employed. R: Patient acknowledges impact. P: Referral to addiction services.
S: Panic attacks reduced to 1/week. O: Breathing is controlled. A: Panic disorder improving. P: Continue exposure therapy.
Comprehensive biopsychosocial assessment completed. Patient has significant trauma history. Diagnoses: PTSD, MDD.
D: First session post-hospitalization. A: Safety planning reviewed and updated. P: Daily check-ins with support person.
S: Sleep improving with sleep hygiene protocol. O: Fatigue less prominent. A: Insomnia secondary to depression. P: Continue CBT-I.
Annual treatment plan review. Goals updated to include vocational rehabilitation. Diagnosis: Bipolar II maintained on lithium.
S: Patient reports good mood stability. O: MSE unremarkable. A: Bipolar II disorder, euthymic. P: Maintenance therapy.
S: Anxiety flare related to job loss. O: Tearful but engaged. A: Adjustment disorder with anxiety. P: Increase session frequency.
Adolescent intake. Parents present. Presents with school refusal and social anxiety.
B: Reported a panic attack at work Monday. I: In-session exposure exercise completed. R: Patient tolerated well. P: Home exposure tasks assigned.
D: Grief processing — 6 months post loss. A: Complicated grief emerging. P: Referral to grief support group.
S: Patient no-showed first 15 minutes. Session abbreviated. O: Appeared disheveled. A: MDD with poor medication adherence. P: Medication reconciliation needed.
S: Reports significant improvement since starting SSRI. O: Brighter affect. A: MDD, partial remission. P: Continue current regimen.
B: Disclosed self-harm 3 weeks ago. I: Safety assessment completed; no current ideation. R: Safety plan updated. P: Weekly sessions, crisis line reviewed.
D: Ongoing caregiver stress managing spouse's dementia. A: Psychoeducation on burnout provided. P: Referral to caregiver support group.
S: Patient doing well. O: Stable. A: PTSD improving. P: Monthly check-ins.
S: Relationship conflict escalating. O: Distressed but cooperative. A: Adjustment disorder. P: Couples therapy referral discussed.
New patient intake. Presenting concern: ADHD reassessment for adult. Referred by family physician.
S: Patient stable. O: MSE normal. A: GAD. P: Continue CBT.
